title: Terrine JWholesome Egyptian Eggplant Gratin Kazan
description: An earthy and aerated brunch dish featuring Egyptian eggplants in a cajun-style gratin with ttukbaegi-earthenware-cook properties, suitable for a variety of dietary needs including fodmap-friendly. Served as an appetizer or side dish.
instructions: Slice the eggplants lengthwise and sprinkle with salt, then drizzle with olive oil. Roast in the preheated kazan for about 25 minutes or until tender.
In a saucepan over medium heat, sauté minced garlic in lemon juice until fragrant. Add heavy cream and cumin; reduce to simmer for about 10 minutes, then season with salt and pepper.
Gently fold the roasted eggplant slices into the cream mixture, ensuring they are evenly coated.
Transfer the mixture back into the kazan-caldron and bake at 180°C for approximately 25-30 minutes or until the top is golden brown and bubbly.
Sprinkle with fresh parsley before serving.
